properties.name: Kansas (Mid-Continent shelf)
properties.target_rocks: Mississippian Lansing-Kansas City limestones, Pennsylvanian Cherokee sandstone, Permian Hugoton dolomite
properties.role: Mature shallow oil and gas; 419,777 wells, mostly vertical, mostly pre-2000
properties.one_line: A century of shallow vertical conventional. The classic dark-data archetype.

properties.name: Permian Basin (West Texas + SE New Mexico)
properties.target_rocks: Wolfcamp Shale, Bone Spring Sand, Spraberry Trend, Avalon Shale, Yeso Limestone
properties.trap_type: Stacked plays in a foreland basin; multiple pay zones per well
properties.role: Largest current US oil producer; multi-zone unconventional + legacy vertical
properties.one_line: The biggest current US oil basin; lit on top of dark vertical legacy.

properties.name: Williston Basin (Bakken & Three Forks TPS)
properties.target_rocks: Bakken Formation (Upper + Middle + Lower), Three Forks dolomite
properties.trap_type: Continuous source-rock shale; the source rock IS the reservoir
properties.role: Bakken horizontal era dominates; one of two majority-lit basins in the series
properties.one_line: Source rock IS reservoir. Horizontal-only era dominates the well count.

properties.name: Anadarko Basin (SCOOP/STACK + Hugoton)
properties.target_rocks: Woodford Shale, Cana-Woodford, Granite Wash, Cleveland Sand, Hugoton dolomite
properties.trap_type: Deep foreland basin (deepest in the series); SCOOP/STACK on the OK side, Hugoton shelf on KS side
properties.role: Deep gas + recent SCOOP/STACK horizontal oil; Hugoton was the largest US gas field for half a century
properties.one_line: Deepest plays in the series. SCOOP/STACK is a thin top layer.

properties.name: Appalachian Basin (Marcellus + Utica + Devonian)
properties.target_rocks: Marcellus Shale, Utica/Point Pleasant, Devonian shales (Berea, Onondaga, Genesee)
properties.trap_type: Foreland basin behind the Allegheny Front; layered source-and-reservoir shales
properties.role: #1 US natural gas producer (Marcellus); 165 years of vertical legacy underneath
properties.one_line: Drake 1859. The longest drilling history in North America.

properties.name: Eagle Ford (USGS AUs across South TX)
properties.target_rocks: Eagle Ford Group (Lower + Upper), Austin Chalk overburden
properties.trap_type: Continuous shale on a south-dipping passive margin; oil-window updip, gas-window downdip
properties.role: Newest big shale; rapid 2008 to 2015 buildup followed by ten-year plateau
properties.one_line: Newest big play. TX RRC architecture means we cannot count its wells.
